level1_segments.summary: The head surgeon performs initial patient preparation.
The head surgeon calibrates the Mako robotic arm and touches the patient with short interruptions.
The head surgeon performs extensive sawing and drilling using the Mako robotic arm and bone saw, interspersed with calibration and brief pauses.

The head surgeon prepares the patient, with brief pauses in between.

level1_segments.summary: The surgical instrument is held by the scrub nurse for an extended initial period.
The instrument is briefly held by the robot technician before returning to the scrub nurse.
The instrument undergoes a series of rapid exchanges between the assistant surgeon and the scrub nurse.
The head surgeon takes primary control of the instrument with intermittent assistance from the scrub nurse and assistant surgeon.
The instrument is transferred back to the assistant surgeon, who holds it for the remainder of the sequence with occasional shared handling.
The surgical instrument is actively held and manipulated by the assistant and head surgeons during a continuous phase of the procedure.
The instrument is transferred to the scrub nurse and then back to the head surgeon for a subsequent task.
The instrument is passed from the robot technician and scrub nurse to the circulator nurse, concluding its active use in this sequence.

level2_segments.summary: The surgical instrument remains in the pre-operative holding phase, managed primarily by the scrub nurse and briefly by the robot technician before active use begins.
The instrument enters its active surgical phase, undergoing rapid exchanges and continuous manipulation by the head surgeon, assistant surgeon, and scrub nurse to perform core procedural tasks.
The instrument's active lifecycle concludes as it is passed from the surgical team to the circulator nurse for final handling.

level1_segments.summary: The scrub nurse repeatedly prepares the instrument table and holds surgical instruments with brief pauses in between.
The scrub nurse manipulates the Mako robotic arm while holding a bone saw.

The scrub nurse continuously manages the instrument table and provides instruments while assisting with patient preparation and the main surgical procedure.

level1_segments.summary: The robotic system undergoes a comprehensive pre-surgery calibration sequence including arm status, homing, accuracy, brake, and angle checks before finalizing the setup.
The surgical team performs detailed bone registration for the femur and tibia, capturing data from various anatomical landmarks and surfaces despite intermittent patient timeouts.
The robotic system initiates a new phase of the procedure following the completion of bone registration.

level2_segments.summary: The robotic system undergoes a comprehensive pre-surgery calibration sequence to verify arm status, homing, accuracy, and angles.
The surgical team performs detailed bone registration for the femur and tibia, followed by the initiation of the next procedural phase.

active_predicates: Dressing the robot, to make it sterile

robot setup (physical): Install the Saw by nurse
robot setup (physical): Install calibration array
robot setup (physical): Remove calibration Array

level1_segments.summary: The robot is powered on to begin the setup process.
Initial calibration of the robot is performed by the MPS.
The robot is dressed to ensure a sterile environment.
A nurse installs the saw onto the robot.
The calibration array is installed and subsequently removed to complete the calibration sequence.

level2_segments.summary: The robot is powered on and undergoes initial calibration to prepare for the procedure.
The robot is dressed for sterility, equipped with a saw, and completes its calibration sequence with an array.
